text:
DISCHARGE SUMMARY

Patient: 67-year-old male
Admission: Outpatient oncology follow-up

Diagnoses:
1. Stage IIIA non-small cell lung cancer, on carboplatin/pemetrexed (C1 completed)
2. Type 2 diabetes mellitus (HbA1c 8.2%, poorly controlled)
3. Essential hypertension (stable)

Course:
Patient seen for follow-up after first cycle of chemotherapy. Reports expected side effects: moderate fatigue, decreased appetite, occasional nausea managed with ondansetron.

Pertinent Labs:
- WBC: 3.2 (low) - monitor for neutropenia
- Platelets: 145 (stable)
- Creatinine: 1.3 (mildly elevated)
- HbA1c: 8.2%
- Fasting glucose: 180-240 mg/dL

Discharge Medications:
1. Metformin 1000mg PO BID
2. Lisinopril 20mg PO daily
3. Ondansetron 8mg PO PRN nausea

Follow-up Instructions:
- Lab recheck (CBC, BMP) in 1 week
- Return to clinic in 2 weeks
- Call immediately if fever >100.4F, bleeding, or severe weakness

Pending: Endocrine evaluation for diabetes management optimization
source_text: Patient: 67-year-old male with Type 2 diabetes mellitus (HbA1c 8.2%), hypertension (on lisinopril 20mg daily), and recently diagnosed stage IIIA non-small cell lung cancer. Patient presents for follow-up after first cycle of carboplatin/pemetrexed chemotherapy. Reports moderate fatigue, decreased appetite, and occasional nausea. Blood glucose readings have been elevated (180-240 mg/dL fasting) since starting chemotherapy. Current medications: metformin 1000mg BID, lisinopril 20mg daily, ondansetron 8mg PRN nausea. Lab results: WBC 3.2 (low), platelets 145, creatinine 1.3 (slightly elevated).
